<!-- markdownlint-disable-file -->

# C# Clean Code — Examples by Rule

The catalog in `SKILL.md` is the index; the worked examples below demonstrate each rule.
Reproduce these patterns when fixing or editing C# code, and cite the matching rule ID.

---

## Comments (C1-C5)

### C1: No Inappropriate Information

Comments should not hold metadata. Use Git for author names, change history,
ticket numbers, and dates.

```csharp
// Bad
// Author: Jane Doe
// Changed: 2024-01-15 — added null check for ticket AB#1234
public User? FindUser(string id) { ... }

// Good — technical context only
// Binary search requires the list to be sorted before calling.
public int Find(IList<int> sorted, int target) { ... }
```

### C2: Delete Obsolete Comments

If a comment describes code that no longer exists or behaves differently,
delete it immediately. Stale comments become sources of confusion.

### C3: No Redundant Comments

```csharp
// Bad — the code already says this
i++; // increment i
await repo.SaveAsync(user); // save the user

// Good — explains WHY, not WHAT
i++; // compensate for zero-indexing in display layer
```

### C4: Write Comments Well

For public API documentation use XML documentation blocks so IDEs and
tooling can surface them. Document intent and non-obvious behavior, not
the signature itself.

```csharp
// Bad — restates the signature
/// <summary>Get user by id.</summary>
public Task<User?> GetUserAsync(string id) { ... }

// Good — documents intent, edge cases, and exceptions
/// <summary>
/// Retrieves a user by their unique identifier.
/// </summary>
/// <param name="userId">The unique identifier of the user to retrieve.</param>
/// <param name="cancellationToken">Token to cancel the operation.</param>
/// <returns>
/// The <see cref="User"/> if found; otherwise, <see langword="null"/>.
/// </returns>
/// <exception cref="ArgumentException">
/// Thrown when <paramref name="userId"/> is empty or whitespace.
/// </exception>
public async Task<User?> GetUserAsync(
    string userId,
    CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)
{
    ArgumentException.ThrowIfNullOrWhiteSpace(userId);
    return await _repository.FindByIdAsync(userId, cancellationToken);
}
```

### C5: Never Commit Commented-Out Code

```csharp
// DELETE THIS — it's an abomination
// private double OldCalculateTax(double income)
// {
//     return income * 0.15;
// }
```

Git remembers everything. Delete it.

---

## Environment (E1-E2)

### E1: One Command to Build

```bash
dotnet build
```

### E2: One Command to Test

```bash
dotnet test
```

---

## Functions / Methods (F1-F4)

### F1: Too Many Arguments (Maximum 3)

```csharp
// Bad — too many parameters
public User CreateUser(
    string name, string email, int age,
    string country, string timezone, bool newsletter) { ... }

// Good — use a record
public record CreateUserRequest(
    string Name,
    string Email,
    int Age,
    string Country,
    string Timezone,
    bool Newsletter);

public User CreateUser(CreateUserRequest request) { ... }
```

### F2: No Output Arguments

Don't modify arguments as side effects. Return values instead. Avoid `ref`/`out`
at public boundaries; prefer tuples or result types.

```csharp
// Bad — mutates the argument
public void AppendFooter(StringBuilder report)
{
    report.AppendLine("---\nGenerated by System");
}

// Good — return the value
public string AppendFooter(string report)
    => report + "\n---\nGenerated by System";
```

### F3: No Flag Arguments

Flag arguments reveal that a method does two things. Split them.

```csharp
// Bad
public void Render(bool isMobile) { ... }

// Good
public void RenderForDesktop() { ... }
public void RenderForMobile() { ... }
```

### F4: Delete Dead Methods

Methods that are never called and not part of a public contract should be deleted.
If it might be needed later, Git remembers it.

---

## General (G1-G36)

### G5: DRY — No Duplication

```csharp
// Bad — same tax logic repeated in two places
public decimal CalculateInvoiceTotal(decimal subtotal)
    => subtotal + subtotal * 0.0825m;

public decimal CalculateReceiptTotal(decimal subtotal)
    => subtotal + subtotal * 0.0825m;

// Good — extracted constant and shared helper
private const decimal TaxRate = 0.0825m;

private static decimal AddTax(decimal subtotal) => subtotal * (1 + TaxRate);

public decimal CalculateInvoiceTotal(decimal subtotal) => AddTax(subtotal);
public decimal CalculateReceiptTotal(decimal subtotal) => AddTax(subtotal);
```

### G9: Delete Dead Code

Remove unused `using` directives, private methods, unreachable branches, and
obsolete properties. Run `dotnet format` and Roslyn analyzers to surface them.

### G16: No Obscured Intent

```csharp
// Bad — what is this doing?
var r = d.Where(x => x.s == 1).Select(x => x.p * 1.0825).Sum();

// Good — obvious at a glance
var taxableItems = orders.Where(o => o.Status == OrderStatus.Taxable);
var totalWithTax = taxableItems.Sum(o => o.Price * (1 + TaxRate));
```

### G19: Use Explanatory Variables

```csharp
// Bad
if (user.Age >= 18 && user.Country == "US" && !user.IsBanned)

// Good
var isEligible = user.Age >= 18 && user.Country == "US" && !user.IsBanned;
if (isEligible)
```

### G23: Prefer Polymorphism to if/else Chains

```csharp
// Bad — open/closed violation; grows with every new type
public decimal CalculateShipping(string type, decimal weight)
{
    if (type == "standard") return weight * 0.5m;
    if (type == "express")  return weight * 1.5m;
    if (type == "overnight") return weight * 3.0m;
    throw new ArgumentException($"Unknown type: {type}");
}

// Good — each strategy is closed for modification
public interface IShippingStrategy
{
    decimal Calculate(decimal weight);
}

public class StandardShipping : IShippingStrategy
{
    public decimal Calculate(decimal weight) => weight * 0.5m;
}

public class ExpressShipping : IShippingStrategy
{
    public decimal Calculate(decimal weight) => weight * 1.5m;
}
```

### G25: Named Constants, Not Magic Numbers

```csharp
// Bad
var total = subtotal * 1.0825;
if (retries > 3) throw new Exception("Too many retries");

// Good
private const double TaxRate = 0.0825;
private const int MaxRetries = 3;

var total = subtotal * (1 + TaxRate);
if (retries > MaxRetries) throw new InvalidOperationException("Too many retries");
```

### G28: Encapsulate Conditionals

```csharp
// Bad
if (user.Age >= 18 && !user.IsBanned && user.HasVerifiedEmail)

// Good
if (IsEligibleUser(user))

private static bool IsEligibleUser(User user)
    => user.Age >= 18 && !user.IsBanned && user.HasVerifiedEmail;
```

### G29: Avoid Negative Conditionals

```csharp
// Bad
if (!isNotReady) { ... }

// Good
if (isReady) { ... }
```

### G30: Methods Do One Thing

```csharp
// Bad — validates, maps, saves, and sends email
public async Task RegisterUserAsync(RegisterRequest request)
{
    if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(request.Email)) throw new ArgumentException("...");
    var user = new User { Name = request.Name, Email = request.Email };
    await _repo.AddAsync(user);
    await _emailService.SendWelcomeAsync(user.Email);
}

// Good — each step is named and isolated
public async Task RegisterUserAsync(RegisterRequest request)
{
    ValidateRequest(request);
    var user = MapToUser(request);
    await _repo.AddAsync(user);
    await _emailService.SendWelcomeAsync(user.Email);
}
```

### G36: Law of Demeter — No Train Wrecks

```csharp
// Bad
var city = order.Customer.Address.City.ToUpper();

// Good — ask the object for what you need
var city = order.GetCustomerCity();
```

---

## C#-Specific (CS1-CS4)

### CS1: File-Scoped Namespaces

```csharp
// Bad — unnecessary block scope adds indentation
namespace Company.Project.Feature
{
    public class UserService { }
}

// Good — file-scoped namespace, no extra indent
namespace Company.Project.Feature;

public class UserService { }
```

Namespace must align with folder structure. `Company.Project.Feature` lives in
`src/Company.Project/Feature/`.

### CS2: Nullable Reference Types

```csharp
// Bad — NRT disabled or bypassed with null-forgiving everywhere
public string Name = null!;
public string? GetName() => null;

// Good — required for non-nullable, ? for truly optional
public class User
{
    public required string Id { get; set; }
    public required string Name { get; set; }
    public string? Nickname { get; set; }  // legitimately optional
}

// Acceptable null-forgiving — framework API without annotations
var header = request.Headers["Authorization"]!;
```

### CS3: XML Documentation

Required on all `public` and `protected` members. Use `<inheritdoc/>` on
implementations and overrides rather than duplicating text.

```csharp
// Bad — missing docs on public member
public class OrderService(IOrderRepository repository)
{
    public async Task<Order?> GetOrderAsync(int orderId, CancellationToken ct)
        => await repository.FindAsync(orderId, ct);
}

// Good
/// <summary>Provides order management operations.</summary>
public class OrderService(IOrderRepository repository)
{
    /// <summary>Retrieves an order by its identifier.</summary>
    /// <param name="orderId">The unique identifier of the order.</param>
    /// <param name="cancellationToken">Token to cancel the operation.</param>
    /// <returns>The <see cref="Order"/> if found; otherwise, <see langword="null"/>.</returns>
    public async Task<Order?> GetOrderAsync(int orderId, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)
        => await repository.FindAsync(orderId, cancellationToken);
}

// Interface implementations use inheritdoc
public class CachedOrderService(IOrderRepository repository) : IOrderService
{
    /// <inheritdoc/>
    public async Task<Order?> GetOrderAsync(int orderId, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)
        => await repository.FindAsync(orderId, cancellationToken);
}
```

### CS4: Records, Primary Constructors, and Pattern Matching

```csharp
// Bad — verbose data class with boilerplate
public class Point
{
    public int X { get; }
    public int Y { get; }
    public Point(int x, int y) { X = x; Y = y; }
}

// Bad — type-check then cast
if (shape is Shape && ((Shape)shape).Color == "red") { ... }

// Good — record with positional syntax
public record Point(int X, int Y);

// Good — primary constructor for services
public class UserService(IUserRepository repository, ILogger<UserService> logger)
{
    public async Task<User?> FindAsync(string id, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        logger.LogInformation("Finding user {UserId}", id);
        return await repository.FindByIdAsync(id, ct);
    }
}

// Good — pattern matching instead of type-check + cast
if (shape is Circle { Color: "red" } circle)
{
    ProcessCircle(circle);
}

// Good — collection expressions
List<string> tags = [..existingTags, "new-tag"];
```

---

## Names (N1-N7)

### N1: Descriptive Names

```csharp
// Bad
var d = DateTime.UtcNow;
List<User> u = new();
bool f = false;

// Good
var createdAt = DateTime.UtcNow;
List<User> activeUsers = new();
bool isEmailVerified = false;
```

### N5: Name Length Matches Scope

```csharp
// Bad — cryptic one-letter variable in wide scope
foreach (var u in userRepository.GetAll())
    await emailService.SendWelcomeAsync(u.Email);

// Bad — overly verbose name in tiny loop
for (var listIterationIndex = 0; listIterationIndex < items.Count; listIterationIndex++)

// Good — short in tight scope, descriptive in wide scope
foreach (var user in userRepository.GetAll())
    await emailService.SendWelcomeAsync(user.Email);

for (var i = 0; i < items.Count; i++)
```

### N6: No Encodings

```csharp
// Bad — Hungarian notation
string strName;
bool bIsActive;
IRepository iRepo;

// Good — type is clear from context
string name;
bool isActive;
IRepository repo;
```

---

## Tests (T1-T9) and C# Test Conventions (CT1-CT5)

### CT1: BDD-Style Test Names

```csharp
// Bad — unclear what is tested and expected
[Fact]
public async Task Test1() { ... }

[Fact]
public async Task ProcessDataAsyncReturnsResult() { ... }

// Good — context, action, and expected outcome are explicit
[Fact]
public async Task WhenValidRequest_ProcessDataAsync_ReturnsParsedResponse() { ... }

[Fact]
public async Task GivenEmptyInput_ProcessDataAsync_ThrowsArgumentException() { ... }
```

### CT2: XUnit + NSubstitute

```csharp
// Create substitutes
var repository = Substitute.For<IUserRepository>();
var logger = Substitute.For<ILogger<UserService>>();

// Configure returns
repository.FindByIdAsync("user-1", Arg.Any<CancellationToken>())
          .Returns(new User { Id = "user-1", Name = "Alice" });

// Verify calls
await repository.Received(1).SaveAsync(Arg.Is<User>(u => u.Name == "Alice"), Arg.Any<CancellationToken>());
repository.DidNotReceive().DeleteAsync(Arg.Any<string>(), Arg.Any<CancellationToken>());
```

### CT3: Arrange/Act/Assert

```csharp
[Fact]
public async Task WhenValidId_GetUserAsync_ReturnsUser()
{
    // Arrange
    var expected = new User { Id = "user-1", Name = "Alice" };
    _repository.FindByIdAsync("user-1", Arg.Any<CancellationToken>())
               .Returns(expected);

    // Act
    var actual = await _sut.GetUserAsync("user-1");

    // Assert
    Assert.Equivalent(expected, actual);
}
```

### CT4: `_sut` Naming and Field Order

```csharp
public class UserServiceTests
{
    // Fields alphabetical by name after underscore — _sut last
    private readonly ILogger<UserService> _logger;
    private readonly IUserRepository _repository;
    private readonly UserService _sut;

    public UserServiceTests()
    {
        _logger = Substitute.For<ILogger<UserService>>();
        _repository = Substitute.For<IUserRepository>();
        _sut = new UserService(_repository, _logger);
    }
}
```

### CT5: One Assertion Per Test

```csharp
// Bad — multiple unrelated assertions
[Fact]
public async Task WhenCreated_UserHasCorrectState()
{
    var user = await _sut.CreateUserAsync("Alice", "alice@example.com");
    Assert.NotNull(user);
    Assert.Equal("Alice", user.Name);
    Assert.Equal("alice@example.com", user.Email);
    Assert.NotEmpty(user.Id);
}

// Good — one behavior per test; use Assert.Equivalent for structural equality
[Fact]
public async Task WhenValidInput_CreateUserAsync_ReturnsExpectedUser()
{
    var expected = new User { Name = "Alice", Email = "alice@example.com" };

    var actual = await _sut.CreateUserAsync("Alice", "alice@example.com");

    Assert.Equivalent(expected, actual, strict: false);
}
```

### T5: Test Boundary Conditions

```csharp
[Theory]
[InlineData("")]
[InlineData("   ")]
[InlineData(null)]
public async Task GivenNullOrWhiteSpaceId_GetUserAsync_ThrowsArgumentException(string? userId)
{
    await Assert.ThrowsAsync<ArgumentException>(
        () => _sut.GetUserAsync(userId!));
}
```

### T9: Tests Must Be Fast (< 100ms each)

- Avoid real I/O in unit tests — use NSubstitute substitutes for repositories and HTTP clients.
- Use `MockHttpMessageHandler` to intercept HTTP calls rather than hitting real endpoints.
- Reserve integration tests (with real infrastructure) for a separate `*.IntegrationTests` project
  and exclude them from the default `dotnet test` run with a filter or category attribute.
